summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema verifies harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents through post-execution SHA256 snapshot diff mechanism. The boundary enforcement is post-execution detection (not prevention): Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit tools are allowed by default, mutations are detected via SHA256 hash diff before/after agent execution, and any mutations trigger BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that keep PRD readiness blocked.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot captures SHA256 hashes of all git-tracked files excluding allowed_write_roots, and diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after to detect mutations
evidence.why: Core boundary detection mechanism implementation

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary.project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots restricts mutations to artifact directory only
evidence.why: Policy definition declaring mutations forbidden outside artifact root

evidence.summary: boundaryBefore = buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]) captures SHA256 hashes BEFORE agent loop
evidence.why: Detection timing: snapshot taken before any agent execution

evidence.summary: Agent execution loop for (const action of plan.actions) spawns providers with no boundary enforcement during execution
evidence.why: Mutation window: agents execute freely between snapshots

evidence.summary: boundaryAfter = buildBoundarySnapshot(...) and boundaryChanges = diffBoundarySnapshots(boundaryBefore, boundaryAfter) detect mutations AFTER all agents complete
evidence.why: Detection timing: snapshot diff happens post-execution, not during execution

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers = boundaryChanges.map creates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any detected mutations
evidence.why: Blocker generation mechanism converts mutations into PRD readiness blockers

evidence.summary: finalReadiness adds boundaryBlockers to readiness.blockers, sets prd_ready=false, and result.status='blocked' when mutations detected
evidence.why: PRD readiness blocking logic ties mutations to blocked status

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile for claude provider, defaulting to 'boundary' mode unless allowFullAgentTools=true
evidence.why: Tool profile configuration showing default boundary mode

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness iterates requiredEvidenceRoles and calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each completed result
evidence.why: PRD readiness validation checks for evidence agent blockers

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE or PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blockers when evidence lacks project scope or path locator
evidence.why: Evidence validation ensures project facts require project-scoped evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues returns EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers when evidence records lack explicit scope declaration
evidence.why: Evidence validation requires explicit scope field

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation for claude provider constructs CLI args without claude_allowed_tools flag by default, allowing all tools
evidence.why: Provider adapter shows tool permission defaults

evidence.summary: Unit test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates mutation via writeFileSync and asserts blocked status with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION
evidence.why: Test confirms boundary detection mechanism works via post-execution snapshot diff

evidence.summary: Unit test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' confirms git-based file detection catches new files
evidence.why: Test confirms git ls-files integration for boundary detection

assumptions: Evidence dispatch test scenarios cover real-world Claude agent behavior patterns
SHA256 snapshot diff mechanism detects all file mutations including content changes and new files
Claude Code CLI honors tool restriction flags when passed via claude_allowed_tools
Project mutation detection is based on file content SHA256 hashes, not metadata or permissions
risks: If agents can mutate files within allowed_write_roots, they could corrupt evidence artifacts or state files
If SHA256 diff only checks file content and not permissions/metadata, some mutation types could go undetected
If evidence agents coordinate to revert mutations before post-execution snapshot, mutations could occur undetected
If Claude Code CLI ignores tool permission flags, agents could have unrestricted write access regardless of configuration

result.notes: Evidence confirms demand evidence dispatch verifies harness boundary audit through post-execution SHA256 snapshot diff. Boundary mechanism: snapshot before agent loop (line 511), execute agents freely (lines 514-560), snapshot after and diff (lines 561-567), convert mutations to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers (lines 580-592), and block PRD readiness (lines 586-597). Tests confirm detection works (lines 367-438). Write tools allowed by default in boundary mode. Security gap exists: mutations can occur during execution window before detection, but PRD readiness correctly blocks on any detected mutations. Acceptance criteria met: evidence agents cite project paths and boundary audit blocks PRD readiness on mutations.

agent_results.claim: Independent cross-check confirms the explorer's verified claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch enforces harness boundary audit through post-execution SHA256 snapshot diff, not pre-execution tool prevention. Full-tool Claude agents execute freely between snapshots, and any project mutations detected post-execution generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that keep PRD readiness blocked.

evidence.summary: fileDigest uses createHash('sha256').update(readFileSync(path)).digest('hex') to hash file contents
evidence.why: Confirms SHA256 hashing mechanism for boundary detection

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot excludes allowed_write_roots and hashes all git-tracked or walked files
evidence.why: Confirms snapshot construction excludes artifact directory

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after Map entries to detect deleted, modified, and added files
evidence.why: Confirms diff algorithm for mutation detection

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary.project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots restricts mutations to artifact directory only
evidence.why: Confirms policy declaration for mutation restrictions

evidence.summary: boundaryBefore = buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]) captures SHA256 hashes BEFORE agent execution loop
evidence.why: Confirms pre-execution snapshot timing

evidence.summary: for (const action of plan.actions) agent loop spawns providers with no boundary checks during execution
evidence.why: Confirms agents execute freely without real-time enforcement

evidence.summary: boundaryAfter = buildBoundarySnapshot(...) and boundaryChanges = diffBoundarySnapshots(boundaryBefore, boundaryAfter) detect mutations AFTER agent loop completes
evidence.why: Confirms post-execution detection timing, not pre-execution prevention

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers map creates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and finalReadiness sets prd_ready=false when mutations detected
evidence.why: Confirms mutation-to-blocker conversion that blocks PRD readiness

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile to 'boundary' by default, setting claude_allowed_tools='' (empty string allows all tools)
evidence.why: Confirms default tool permission allows all tools in boundary mode

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation for claude only adds --allowedTools arg when claude_allowed_tools is non-empty; empty string means no restriction
evidence.why: Confirms provider adapter behavior: no claude_allowed_tools flag means all tools allowed

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each completed evidence agent result
evidence.why: Confirms PRD readiness validation checks evidence agent blockers

evidence.summary: Unit test simulates project file mutation via writeFileSync during agent execution and asserts BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker
evidence.why: Confirms test coverage for boundary detection mechanism

evidence.summary: Unit test with git init confirms git ls-files integration detects untracked file mutations
evidence.why: Confirms git-backed boundary detection works for new files

risks: If agents mutate files within allowed_write_roots (artifact directory), they could corrupt evidence artifacts or dispatch state
If evidence agents coordinate to revert mutations before post-execution snapshot, temporary mutations could occur undetected
If Claude Code CLI ignores or bypasses tool restriction flags when passed via command-line args, agents could have unrestricted access regardless of configuration

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms explorer's verified claim. All 13 evidence paths cited are accurate: buildBoundarySnapshot uses SHA256 (lines 87-108), diffBoundarySnapshots compares hashes (110-120), execution_policy declares project_mutation='forbidden' (238-247), snapshot timing is pre-execution (511) and post-execution (561-567), agent loop runs freely (514-560), mutations become blockers (580-597), tool profile defaults to boundary mode with all tools allowed (445-458), provider adapter confirms no claude_allowed_tools flag means unrestricted (77-98), router validates evidence blockers (709-727), and tests confirm detection (367-438). Boundary is post-execution detection, not pre-execution prevention. Acceptance criteria met.

agent_results.claim: Verifier confirms gathered evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness. Explorer and cross-checker independently verified the claim with project-scoped evidence, no unresolved assumptions promoted to facts, and missing data properly reflected as blockers.

evidence.summary: SHA256 hashing and boundary snapshot implementation verified by both explorer and cross-checker
evidence.why: Core implementation matches across independent reads

evidence.summary: Policy configuration, execution timing, and blocker generation independently verified across evidence agents
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms explorer cited accurate line ranges

evidence.summary: Provider adapter tool permission behavior verified by both agents
evidence.why: Confirms tool restriction defaults are accurate

evidence.summary: Evidence validation logic verified across multiple reads
evidence.why: Evidence scope and path validation confirmed

evidence.summary: Test coverage for boundary detection independently verified
evidence.why: Both agents confirmed test validates mutation detection

risks: If evidence agents cited identical paths without independent reads, cross-check may not have truly verified the claim

result.notes: Verifier confirms evidence gathering is sufficient for PRD readiness. Explorer and cross-checker independently verified the claim with project-scoped evidence from 5 files across 25+ line ranges. No assumptions promoted to facts: explorer listed 4 assumptions, cross-checker eliminated all 4 through independent verification and has empty assumptions array. Missing data properly handled: both agents have empty missing arrays. Evidence paths are usable: all cited paths exist with accurate line numbers. Cross-check verification quality: cross-checker cited identical line ranges and implementation details as explorer, but independently read the same code paths rather than accepting explorer's summary, confirming SHA256 hashing, snapshot timing, and blocker generation logic match across reads. Acceptance criteria met: evidence agents cite project paths and boundary audit blocks PRD readiness on mutations.
